title: Boat Rental in Estepona Port 2026: Costs, Seasons & Tips
html:
Estepona Port, on the Costa del Sol, serves as a vibrant departure point for Mediterranean voyages, with weekly prices for bareboat powerboats starting around 1,200 EUR in high season. BoatHire24 connects you to verified options across this Andalusian harbour.

Where to set off in Estepona Port

Depart from Puerto Deportivo de Estepona, a well-sheltered marina with full facilities. From here you can head to Cabo Pino Marina (4 nm east), Puerto Banús (15 nm), or Marbella’s harbour (20 nm). To the west, the Bay of Gibraltar (30 nm) and Sotogrande (28 nm) are accessible. The nearby offshore island of Isla de las Palomas (just 1 nm south) is a popular anchoring spot.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motor Yacht: Ideal for day trips or coastal cruising, accommodating 6, 10 guests, from 1,200 EUR per half-day. For a flagship model, see the Prestige 500 charter page for details.
  2. Sailing Catamaran: Spacious and stable, perfect for families or groups of 8, 12, from 2,000 EUR per full day.
  3. RIB or Tender: Fast and nimble for water sports or short excursions, carrying 4, 6 guests, from 400 EUR for two hours.
  4. Luxury Yacht (e.g., Azimut 68): Full-day crewed charters with 8, 12 guests, from 4,000 EUR.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Estepona Port?

Daily charter costs range from 400 EUR for a small RIB to over 4,000 EUR for a crewed luxury yacht.

  • Shared tour: 60, 100 EUR per person for a group sailing experience.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): 400, 800 EUR self-drive.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): 2,000, 3,000 EUR with skipper.
  • Crewed yacht (full day, 10, 12 guests): 3,500, 5,000 EUR including crew and fuel.

You can compare prices and book directly through skippered charters in Estepona Port on BoatHire24, which often include insurance and captain.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Estepona Port?

Without a valid Spanish licence or recognised international certificate, you must charter a skippered boat. Spanish law requires a licencia de navegación for boats over 6 metres or engines exceeding 15 kW. The operator will confirm the exact requirements before booking. Most holidaymakers prefer a skippered charter to avoid paperwork.

Local tip: book a skippered charter for the first day to learn the local mooring zones near Estepona Port, especially around Cabo Pino, where the wind can shift quickly in the afternoon.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Estepona Port?

Peak season runs from June to September, with July and August offering the warmest weather and light winds. The prevailing wind is the Poniente, a westerly that often brings calm seas in the morning. May and October provide quieter conditions and lower prices. Plan your trip around 2026 if you want to avoid the peak crowds.

At a glance

  • Price range: 400, 5,000 EUR per day
  • Peak months: June, September
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Guest capacity: 4, 12 persons
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Estepona Port

Can I rent a boat for one day in Estepona?

Yes, half-day (4 hours) and full-day (8 hours) rentals are standard, with many boats available for a single day.

Are there boats with a captain available?

Most charters in Estepona Port offer a skipper included in the price; you can also hire a captain separately.

What happens if the weather is bad on my booked day?

The captain or operator will postpone or cancel for safety, often offering a full refund or alternative date.

If you own a boat in Estepona, you can list it to reach thousands of travellers. BoatHire24 makes it simple to compare and book verified boats for 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Estepona Port and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
